[](https://weld2020.nationbuilder.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_3F5VNQquXw1tKM69PB7U23HvggvoZ_zOOINCNDzJ03kY9fRhi-ZgWDwlpLRkGMKu4BmgX0O60zlFgM78zdS4jo&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20191110_fr_s&n=1) Team — you’ve come through big time this weekend. You’ve given us hope that we can keep our ads online through the end of the month, but we’re not quite where we need to be yet. [**We need to raise another $5,000 before Tuesday if we’re going to keep them going. Do you think you can donate now and help us close the gap?**](https://weld2020.nationbuilder.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_3F5VNQquXw1tKM69PB7U23HvggvoZ_zOOINCNDzJ03kY9fRhi-ZgWDwlpLRkGMKu4BmgX0O60zlFgM78zdS4jo&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20191110_fr_s&n=2) | | [DONATE ⇨](https://weld2020.nationbuilder.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_3F5VNQquXw1tKM69PB7U23HvggvoZ_zOOINCNDzJ03kY9fRhi-ZgWDwlpLRkGMKu4BmgX0O60zlFgM78zdS4jo&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20191110_fr_s&n=3)

— Bill

**From** : Team Weld <info@weld2020.org>
**Date** : November 09, 2019
**To** : dpwillis67@gmail.com
[](https://weld2020.nationbuilder.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_3F5VNQquXw1tKM69PB7U23HvggvoZ_zOOINCNDzJ03kY9fRhi-ZgWDwlpLRkGMKu4BmgX0O60zlFgM78zdS4jo&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20191110_fr_s&n=4)

[**Can you give us a hand a rush a donation today to help us keep our ads going?**](https://weld2020.nationbuilder.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_3F5VNQquXw1tKM69PB7U23HvggvoZ_zOOINCNDzJ03kY9fRhi-ZgWDwlpLRkGMKu4BmgX0O60zlFgM78zdS4jo&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20191110_fr_s&n=5)

Team Weld

**Date** : November 08, 2019
**To** : dpwillis67@gmail.com
[](https://weld2020.nationbuilder.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_3F5VNQquXw1tKM69PB7U23HvggvoZ_zOOINCNDzJ03kY9fRhi-ZgWDwlpLRkGMKu4BmgX0O60zlFgM78zdS4jo&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20191110_fr_s&n=7)
Hey Team — before you head out for the holiday. My digital team just let me know that we are being wildly outspent by President Trump on digital ads.
**Donald Trump spent over $2.7 MILLION in digital ads just between October 12th and November 2nd, according to the folks who track that sort of thing. He’s using his fundraising advantages to buy up ad space in hopes that folks won’t see ours.**
Luckily, we’ve got a dedicated group of supporters like you who have been helping us keep our ads going, but we’re not going to lie — our ads budget is getting pretty depleted trying to keep up with the president.
[**Do you think you could give us a hand and help us replenish our ads budget by donating $20.20, or whatever you can, today to help us keep our ads going?**](https://weld2020.nationbuilder.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_3F5VNQquXw1tKM69PB7U23HvggvoZ_zOOINCNDzJ03kY9fRhi-ZgWDwlpLRkGMKu4BmgX0O60zlFgM78zdS4jo&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20191110_fr_s&n=8)****

Thanks,
Bill

Amazon is the largest company in the world, with annual profits of billions of dollars. But while working families across America paid their fair share during tax season to fund roads and bridges... | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/28528885/45c30650/1049074e5/66f5a8e5/831884788/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")

Yesterday, it was reported that Jeff Bezos -- CEO of Amazon and the richest man in the world -- asked Michael Bloomberg if he would consider running for President.

**This is what happens when you threaten to root out the corruption that has taken our government hostage. The wealthy and well-connected will always have each other 's backs if it means having a Washington that caters to their every need.

Because we're running this campaign the right way, Elizabeth won't call up a few wealthy donors and ask them to cut a few big checks. Our campaign is powered by grassroots donors like you.

[We want to fight back, but we're going to need your help -- will you chip in $3 to help fight back against Michael Bloomberg, Jeff Bezos, and the billionaires who want to keep the status quo?](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/28528885/45c30650/1049074e5/66f5a8e4/831884788/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LW5vdjE5ZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IkVNTDQxRl9FR1JfQ09SX0NfMjAxOTExMTBfVjFTM19QX1QxIn0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://secure.elizabethwarren.com/donate/ew-nov19fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=EML41F_EGR_COR_C_20191110_V1S3_P_T1")** | _If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically:_

>
In 2018, Amazon paid zero dollars in federal taxes.

You read that right. _Zero_.

Amazon is the largest company in the world, with annual profits of billions of dollars. But while working families across America paid their fair share during tax season to fund roads and bridges and public schools, Amazon was able to avoid paying a single dollar towards funding the services that all Americans rely on.

How? It's because of a rigged system that favors the wealthy and well-connected.

That's how Amazon was able to avoid taxes in 2018, but they were also able to pay their workers less than a living wage. It's how they were able to cut health care benefits for part-time employees.

**That ends in a Warren Administration.** Elizabeth's Real Corporate Profit Tax will ensure that our biggest and most profitable corporations can never make billions and pay $0 in taxes again. And she has a plan to break up large tech monopolies like Amazon, because no one corporation should have that much influence over our democracy.

So it's no wonder billionaires like Jeff Bezos are making calls to urge like-minded billionaires to run for president themselves. They want a friend in the Oval Office that will cater to their every need -- and they know Elizabeth won't be that person.

**We can win in 2020 and bring big, structural change in 2021. But it isn 't about just one person, and Elizabeth can't do it alone. It's going to take all of us to build a movement to end the stranglehold corporations have, and we need your help.**

[**Can you chip in $3 or anything you can to show Elizabeth you're in this fight with her to hold corporations like Amazon accountable and put power in the hands of working families?**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/28528885/45c30650/1049074e5/66f5a8e4/831884788/VEsEBg/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LW5vdjE5ZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IkVNTDQxRl9FR1JfQ09SX0NfMjAxOTExMTBfVjFTM19QX1QxIn0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://secure.elizabethwarren.com/donate/ew-nov19fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=EML41F_EGR_COR_C_20191110_V1S3_P_T1")

Thanks for everything you do,

Team Warren

[**DONATE NOW**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/28528885/45c30650/1049074e5/66f5a8e4/831884788/VEsEAQ/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LW5vdjE5ZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IkVNTDQxRl9FR1JfQ09SX0NfMjAxOTExMTBfVjFTM19QX1QxIn0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://secure.elizabethwarren.com/donate/ew-nov19fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=EML41F_EGR_COR_C_20191110_V1S3_P_T1")

name: Booker HQ
subject: FWD: How we're going to get Cory on the December debate stage
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
| | |
---
| Team -- Did you see Addisu's message? We've been crunching the numbers here at Booker HQ, and **we need to raise $550,000 by 11:59 p.m. on November 15th** to fully fund our plan to get Cory on the December debate stage. **Will you chip in to our Ground Game Fund today? name: Ed Markey
subject: Just finished the debate
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Markey Committee
body:
| |
---
| Team: I just got off the debate stage at Stonehill College, and one thing is clear: Grassroots Democrats are more energized than ever to pass a Green New Deal and transform our economy and democracy.

Meanwhile, in the face of our greatest planetary crisis, Donald Trump and the Republicans have chosen to do nothing.

Their inaction is criminal.

That's why I'm fighting every day for a Green New Deal and set our country on a course to combat the climate crisis.

_**[I need your help in this fight. Will you pitch in $27 or more and help us reach our $25,000 goal before Monday morning? We're 90% of the way there, and a donation of $27 or more will go a long way to helping us fight. ](https://ma.edmarkey.%3D%3D/?g=-NfBFVNw52hcsCx35ezxeoA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/markey-november2019-weekly?refcode=EM-111019_2&amount=27&recurring=true&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")**_

name: Team Yang
subject: We need you to help close our fundraising gap
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang
body:
We have to hit this goal if we’re going to run our ad for another week and reach important voters in Iowa.

| [](http://act.yang2020.com/go/68?t=1&akid=2968%2E377263%2EjN6bIa) Yang Gang --

The Yang Gang is known for defying the odds. You’ve made sure that we’ve made it to every single debate stage. You’ve worked hard to ensure every fundraising goal has been met. And you’re the reason that we’re running an unprecedented campaign funded almost entirely by a grassroots movement.

[Now we’re facing a $1 million fundraising goal that we must reach by Wednesday. And we’re 8% behind where we should be -- will you pitch in and be the reason we hit this goal?](https://act.yang2020.com/go/16514?t=5&akid=2968%2E377263%2EjN6bIa) _If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [$10 »](https://act.yang2020.com/go/16514?t=13&express_lane=true&amount=10&akid=2968%2E377263%2EjN6bIa)
---
| | [$25 »](https://act.yang2020.com/go/16514?t=12&express_lane=true&amount=25&akid=2968%2E377263%2EjN6bIa)
---
| [$50 »](https://act.yang2020.com/go/16514?t=11&express_lane=true&amount=50&akid=2968%2E377263%2EjN6bIa)
---
| | [$100»](https://act.yang2020.com/go/16514?t=10&express_lane=true&amount=100&akid=2968%2E377263%2EjN6bIa)
---
| [$250 »](https://act.yang2020.com/go/16514?t=9&express_lane=true&amount=250&akid=2968%2E377263%2EjN6bIa)
---
| | [OTHER »](https://act.yang2020.com/go/16514?t=8&amount=25&akid=2968%2E377263%2EjN6bIa)
---
We made this goal public because we believe in the passion and heart of the Yang Gang, and we know that with your help, we will hit this goal like we have all other goals before.

But this time, the stakes are higher. There are a lot more eyes on us, looking to see if we fail. We **have** to hit this goal if we’re going to run our ad for another week and reach important voters in Iowa.

**You have tirelessly built our Humanity First movement from the ground up. And we can hit this goal if we all step up together. That’s why we’re asking each one of you to contribute today to close the 8% gap:**
[Contribute](https://act.yang2020.com/go/16514?t=7&akid=2968%2E377263%2EjN6bIa)
---
Keeping our ad up will allow us reach tens of thousands of voters in Iowa and get to the debate stage in December. If we go dark, we may not make it there.

Let’s hit this goal, continue to introduce voters across the country to Andrew, and spread the importance of our Humanity First values.

Let’s do this,

Team Yang
**We don't have corporate PACs or big donors who can close the fundraising gap we face, just grassroots donors like you powering this campaign. Please give now.** | [Contribute](https://act.yang2020.com/go/16514?t=6&akid=2968%2E377263%2EjN6bIa)
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We're proud you're part of the Yang Gang! But if you want to receive less email from us, you can do that [here.](https://act.yang2020.com/signup/email-actionkit-feweremails-20190821/?t=2&akid=2968%2E377263%2EjN6bIa) And if you absolutely don't want to hear from us again you can [unsubscribe here.](https://act.yang2020.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=3&akid=2968%2E377263%2EjN6bIa) Contributions or gifts to Friends of Andrew Yang are not tax deductible. | Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang
---
© 2019 Friends of Andrew Yang
All Rights Reserved
New York, NY

name: Tom Steyer
subject: I’m running for president to fix our broken government and reunite our country
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TOM STEYER 2020]()
body:
| | | |
---
---
Friend, I just finished my CNN town hall in Iowa where I answered questions from voters about the serious challenges our nation faces. The simple truth is that the structures we rely on are broken. Too many American families are struggling to put food on the table. The climate crisis threatens our homes and our livelihoods. The cost of our health care system is bankrupting families at their most vulnerable moment. Our government is beholden to corporate interests — and our leaders aren’t representing the will of the people. **The work we have ahead of us can seem daunting — but I firmly believe in the power of the American people.** I am grateful for this opportunity to share a bit about my parents, where I’ve come from, and what my vision is for our country’s future. In Washington, our lawless president will continue his efforts to divide us, but with our unified voice, we can fix our broken government. I’m hopeful about the changes we can make together because I know that when the American people choose to do something, there’s nothing that can stop us. I’m running for president to restore dignity to the office and defeat Donald Trump — and everything he stands for. I’m running to reunite our country on the path to progress. **If you’re with me for the next phase of this campaign, will you make a $1 investment tonight? clean_body: | | | | Friend, I just finished my CNN town hall in Iowa where I answered questions from voters about the serious challenges our nation faces. The simple truth is that the structures we rely on are broken. Too many American families are struggling to put food on the table. The climate crisis threatens our homes and our livelihoods. The cost of our health care system is bankrupting families at their most vulnerable moment. Our government is beholden to corporate interests — and our leaders aren’t representing the will of the people. The work we have ahead of us can seem daunting — but I firmly believe in the power of the American people. I am grateful for this opportunity to share a bit about my parents, where I’ve come from, and what my vision is for our country’s future. In Washington, our lawless president will continue his efforts to divide us, but with our unified voice, we can fix our broken government. I’m hopeful about the changes we can make together because I know that when the American people choose to do something, there’s nothing that can stop us. I’m running for president to restore dignity to the office and defeat Donald Trump — and everything he stands for. I’m running to reunite our country on the path to progress. If you’re with me for the next phase of this campaign, will you make a $1 investment tonight? Your grassroots support is valued and important. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | With gratitude, Tom Steyer | [PAID FOR BY TOM STEYER 2020]() We want to make sure you're receiving relevant information about this movement. name: =?utf-8?Q?Juli=C3=A1n_Castro?=
subject: MSNBC: reorder the primaries
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Julian for the Future
body:
| | |
---
| |
---
| |
Tonight on MSNBC, I was asked about the order of our primaries. I told them that I appreciate how seriously Iowa and New Hampshire take their role as first-in-the-nation. Throughout my travels in both states, I have met many wonderful people who take this process very seriously. They vet the candidates, they show up at town halls, and do their due diligence. However, our primary process should reflect our country and the voters in it. What made sense 50 years ago when this order was established no longer works today. We are not the same country that we were in 1972 and I believe it's time our primaries reflect our nation's diversity. **[_DONATE >> _](https://action.julianforthefuture.%3D/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/julian-em?refcode=20191108_em_fr_b2_bjchq_all&recurring=auto&amount=5&amounts=25,50,100,250&utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=1+-+DONATE+gtgt+&utm_campaign=20191110_em_fr_b3_castro_all_internal&source=20191110_em_fr_b3_castro_all_internal")** Other states should have their chance in helping to determine who the nominee of our party - and who the leader of our country should be. I've made a point on this campaign of speaking my mind and giving a voice to the voiceless, even when it makes powerful interests uncomfortable. I know there are some who may disagree, but I think it's time we have an honest conversation about our primaries. We've worked hard to build a campaign that reflects this entire country, help us keep building that now: _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | **[**Chip in $5 now >> **](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fc81a/3eb3e2fc/56eb1c25/744d9269/4164303616/VEsE/p/eyJlM3RoYlc5MWJuUjlmUT09IjoiNSJ9/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/julian-em?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=20191108_em_fr_b2_bjchq_all&utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=2+-+Chip+in+amount+now+gtgt+&utm_campaign=20191110_em_fr_b3_castro_all_internal&source=20191110_em_fr_b3_castro_all_internal")**

No one like Julian Castro has ever been in the Oval Office. His grandmother immigrated here from Mexico when she was just 7 years old. She never got past the 4th grade, but she provided for her family by working as a maid and a housekeeper.

Now, her grandson is running for President of the United States. Against all odds, grassroots Democrats like YOU helped him qualify for the Democratic debates and sent him to the front of the pack of Democratic candidates.

We know we send you a lot of emails, but that's because we're relying on grassroots supporters to overcome our next big challenge:

Julian must be able to compete and build a movement that lasts for the long haul, and he's not taking a cent of corporate PAC money to do it. Will you be a part of this historic campaign?

**[DONATE](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fc81a/3eb3e2fc/56eb1c25/744d9251/4164303616/VEsHBQ/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/julian-em?refcode=email_footer_donate&amount=25&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on&utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=10+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=20191110_em_fr_b3_castro_all_internal&source=20191110_em_fr_b3_castro_all_internal")**
Again, we know we send you a lot of emails. We hope you'll stick with us long-term. If you want to stay updated but receive fewer emails, please let us know [here](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fc81a/3eb3e2fc/56eb1c25/744d9250/4164303616/VEsHBA/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=11+-+here&utm_campaign=20191110_em_fr_b3_castro_all_internal&source=20191110_em_fr_b3_castro_all_internal"). If you'd like to unsubscribe completely, we understand. Please unsubscribe [here](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fc81a/3eb3e2fc/56eb1c25/744d925f/4164303616/VEsHBw/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=12+-+here&utm_campaign=20191110_em_fr_b3_castro_all_internal&source=20191110_em_fr_b3_castro_all_internal"), and we'll make sure to process your request.

clean_body: | | | | | | | Tonight on MSNBC, I was asked about the order of our primaries. I told them that I appreciate how seriously Iowa and New Hampshire take their role as first-in-the-nation. Throughout my travels in both states, I have met many wonderful people who take this process very seriously. They vet the candidates, they show up at town halls, and do their due diligence. However, our primary process should reflect our country and the voters in it. What made sense 50 years ago when this order was established no longer works today. We are not the same country that we were in 1972 and I believe it's time our primaries reflect our nation's diversity. "" Other states should have their chance in helping to determine who the nominee of our party - and who the leader of our country should be. I've made a point on this campaign of speaking my mind and giving a voice to the voiceless, even when it makes powerful interests uncomfortable. I know there are some who may disagree, but I think it's time we have an honest conversation about our primaries. We've worked hard to build a campaign that reflects this entire country, help us keep building that now: If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | "" "" "" "" "" Let's do things differently, Julian | | | | | | | "" | | "" | | "" | | julianforthefuture.com | | Paid for by Julian for the Future | Contributions or gifts to Julian for the Future are not tax deductible. name: Mary Mancini
subject: Welcome the TNDP Veterans & Military Families Caucus
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
Dear Peter,

It's VETERANS DAY 2019.

Every day we remember the dedication and great sacrifice of those who currently serve in our military. Every year we set aside today, Nov. 11, to honor all of the men and women who’ve served in our armed forces from generations past.

Tennessee is home to almost 500,000 veterans and today, the Tennessee Democratic Party, working with our newly formed Veterans and Military Families Caucus [https://tndp.org/veterans-caucus/] , call on every Tennessean to remember the sacrifices made by our armed forces and their families. We hope you will join us as we honor those who have fiercely protected our democratic values of freedom, equality, and justice for all.

Whether serving in combat or stationed overseas or here at home, our veterans have kept us safe and secure and they deserve our words of thanks and our gratitude through our deeds. We must ensure, through good governance and smart policy, that our veterans have a life-time of quality healthcare, meaningful and sustainable employment, affordable housing, and a secure and dignified retirement. Just as our military leaves no one behind on the battlefield, we must leave none of these heros behind at home .

As a daughter and granddaughter of veterans, I’m honored to work side by side with the many brave servicemen and women in the Tennessee Democratic Party and our Veterans and Military Families Caucus. To you, and all of Tennessee’s veterans, thank you for your patriotism, your service, and your sacrifice. As you celebrate with family and friends, please accept our humble thanks for your selfless work and the significant commitment you’ve made to our country.

If you are interested in joining the TNDP Veteran & Military Families Caucus, sign up here [https://tndp.org/veterans-caucus/] .

Thank you,

Mary
--
Mary Mancini
Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party

WHAT'S NEXT? A SPECIAL ELECTION
FOR TENNESSEE STATE HOUSE 77

On Thursday, December 19, voters in Tennessee State House District 77 (Dyer, Lake, and part of Obion County) will go to the polls in a special election to fill a vacant seat.

Democratic candidate Mike Smith is ready to fight for the voters of District 77 because he believes the rural way of life is worth fighting for. He believes that the people of West Tennessee have a right to their roots, a quality public education, and affordable healthcare.

Mike Smith knows that it's time beyond time for a state rep in West Tennessee who will listen more than he talks and will work across the aisle to solve problems instead of always blaming others. Mike will never engage in bullying or name calling, but instead will spend his time addressing the challenges that face West Tennessee.

This special election is all about turnout and it will take many conversations with voters to let voters know when and where they can vote.

HELP ELECT MIKE SMITH! The TNDP has set up a virtual phone bank so that you can help to GET OUT THE VOTE from anywhere in the state (or the country!).

It's so easy. Virtual Phone Bank instructions can be found here [https://s3.amazonaws.com/collective-agency-wp-uploads/wp-content/uploads/sites/5/2019/11/HD77-Virtual-Phone-Bank-Document.pdf] . Access the virtual phone bank here [https://www.openvpb.com/vpb_bycode/F80823P-635276] .

If you are interested in canvassing and phone banking in the district or would like to make a donation, contact campaign manager: Deane Arganbright at argandeane@yahoo.com [argandeane@yahoo.com] or 731-588-0681.

DNC ELECTION UNITY PROGRAM

With one year until the 2020 presidential election, the DNC announced a new initiative to ensure Democrats enter the general election unified behind our eventual nominee and ready to defeat Trump in 2020.

The unity initiative will help ensure that the eventual nominee has the full support of all elements of the party and facilitate the transition of primary campaigns’ infrastructure into supporting Democrats’ general election efforts.

Leading up to the general election, we’ll need every Democrat working together in order to defeat Donald Trump. That’s why the DNC is proud to partner with our presidential candidates and Democratic state parties in this effort so that our eventual nominee has the full support of the entire party behind them. By coming together, we will put our eventual nominee in the strongest possible position to win the White House and help Democrats secure victories at every level of the ballot in 2020.

More info to follow.

ELECTION NEWS: 358 DAYS TO GO

Last Tuesday's elections should send shivers down the spines of Donald Trump and Every Republican running in 2020. Here’s a few key takeaways:

“ The suburban revolt against President Donald Trump’s Republican Party is growing [https://apnews.com/03e78ab61fd94074b49bcfbf73ba4f84] .” Independent-minded, suburban voters continued [https://thehill.com/homenews/campaign/467944-trump-faces-severe-suburban-slump] to shift sharply against Trump.

The AP wrote the elections “ will test voter enthusiasm and party organization [https://time.com/5718614/elections-clues-2020-impeachment/] .” The Democratic victories speak for themselves.

Vox: Winner: Democratic organization and enthusiasm [https://www.vox.com/2019/11/5/20949741/winners-and-losers-election-night-2019] . “Virginia and Kentucky demonstrated one clear thing: Democrats were fired up. No matter what caveats exist in each state, that’s great news for the Democratic Party heading into 2020.”

The Cure for Democrats' 2020 Terror: Building the Blue Wall [https://www.nytimes.com/2019/11/08/opinion/2020-election-democrats.html]

The Washington Post: Could Trump be in trouble with rural voters? [https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2019/11/07/could-trump-be-trouble-with-rural-voters/]

[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/77134/images/whenweallvote.png]

Former first lady (and person we miss in the White House more than anyone) Michelle Obama along with her squad - Tom Hanks, Lin-Manuel Miranda, Janelle Monae, Chris Paul, Faith Hill and Tim McGraw - announced "When We All Vote" and invited you to join! This voter registration program is a unique way for you to get involved either with your county party or with your own squad.

This Wednesday, the When We All Vote team will be holding a kickoff call to let you know about the ways you can get involved and impact your community. They'll walk through volunteer opportunities and discuss their new Voting Squad program for those who are ready to step up and lead in their communities.

Sign up here to join the 2020 kickoff call this Wednesday, November 13 at 7:00 CT / 8:00 p.m. ET. [https://register.gotowebinar.com/register/4516267503519556365?utm_medium=email&utm_source=whenwevote&utm_content=1+-+RSVP+NOW&utm_campaign=email_20191108_VJ_kickoffcallrsvp_all&source=email_20191108_VJ_kickoffcallrsvp_all&refcode=email_20191108_VJ_kickoffcallrsvp_all]

TNDP JOB OPENINGS

In case you missed it, the Tennessee Democratic Party is accepting applications for a Communications Director and a Data Administrator. More information at TNDP.org/jobs [https://tndp.org/jobs/] or by emailing TNDP executive director Kris Murphy at kris@tndp.org [kris@tndp.org?subject=Job%20Inquiry] .

name: Presidential Candidate Joe Sestak
subject: Let's hit the streets!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Joe Sestak for President
body:
Peter,
It's [crunch time](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/11750378/120040232/-571370406?emci=aa11a108-e4fb-e911-828b-2818784d6d68&emdi=85c71d4a-08fc-e911-828b-2818784d6d68&ceid=36399&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS83NTY5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNmMxZDJmYy05MzA0LWVhMTEtODI4Yi0yODE4Nzg0ZDZkNjgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NF-jUpijZ5vJ-CYRMmvub-qcj1D0yFzLXi3tRqFUG60=) for my campaign!
Thanks to some wonderful volunteers who really rose to the challenge, on Friday we filed petitions with **1149 signatures **to secure a spot on the Alabama ballot (they have the first filing deadline). We needed at least 500 signers, and our goal was 1000, so we're very pleased.
But Alabama is** just the beginning**. While not every state requires signatures, plenty do. Next we'll need to [circulate petitions](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/11750379/120040235/-571370406?emci=aa11a108-e4fb-e911-828b-2818784d6d68&emdi=85c71d4a-08fc-e911-828b-2818784d6d68&ceid=36399&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS83NTY5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNmMxZDJmYy05MzA0LWVhMTEtODI4Yi0yODE4Nzg0ZDZkNjgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NF-jUpijZ5vJ-CYRMmvub-qcj1D0yFzLXi3tRqFUG60=) in **Virginia, Vermont, Maine, Illinois, Indiana, Ohio, New York, New Jersey, Delaware, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, Montana, and Washington.**
We can only complete this mission with your help. [Please fill out our **volunteer form**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/11750380/120040239/-571370406?emci=aa11a108-e4fb-e911-828b-2818784d6d68&emdi=85c71d4a-08fc-e911-828b-2818784d6d68&ceid=36399&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS83NTY5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNmMxZDJmYy05MzA0LWVhMTEtODI4Yi0yODE4Nzg0ZDZkNjgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NF-jUpijZ5vJ-CYRMmvub-qcj1D0yFzLXi3tRqFUG60=) (if you haven't already) to help us collect all the signatures we need, especially if you live in one of these states or might be willing to travel.
From talking with voters across the country, it's very clear to me that there remains **a huge opening in this race**. If we can continue to gain some traction in the media, if more people learn about who I am and my vision for the country, and if we get on the ballot in every state, **I have no doubt we can surprise everyone**.
Thank you for believing in me and in this campaign.
Warmly,
Joe

Paid for by Joe Sestak for President.

Alexandria, VA 22302
United States

name: Eugene DePasquale
subject: Recognizing our veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by DePasquale for PA 10
body:
Friend, Today is Veteran’s Day, so I wanted to make sure to take a moment to pay respects to those who have served our country in uniform. To all the active service members, veterans, and military families, I thank you deeply for your service. Your patriotism, love of country, and willingness to make sacrifices for the greater good do not go unnoticed or unappreciated.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/11751123/120053122/1960319551?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NTE3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NGJlNmJjYi05NTA0LWVhMTEtODI4Yi0yODE4Nzg0ZDZkNjgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_SvmNLJblFmtBoYB_VixwU7AItNK1nxoApSHbpUZf48=&emci=1543d6df-6b02-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&emdi=54be6bcb-9504-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&ceid=10203249)
Friend,

Today is Veteran’s Day, so I wanted to make sure to take a moment to pay respects to those who have served our country in uniform. To all the active service members, veterans, and military families, I thank you deeply for your service. Your patriotism, love of country, and willingness to make sacrifices for the greater good do not go unnoticed or unappreciated.

On this day, we must put aside our differences and come together as Americans — honoring the bravery and sacrifice made by those who put their lives on the line for the freedoms we so often take for granted. And we must remain united in ensuring that our veterans and their families receive the resources and care they deserve when they come home.

We are eternally grateful for your service.

God bless the United States of America,
Eugene
---

name: Sri Preston Kulkarni
subject: True courage and patriotism
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sri for Congress
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/dsa0Yt19-4My3bQbKxdPs4X8H1_c06WV7wNRrLi5HHk=/HDT5ixXaHUQ3G2RXpIHUFWZri-608WpXBbIJqGWBhuM=?refcode=refcode=20191111email&amount=22&recurring=auto)
---
Team, Today is Veterans Day -- a time to honor veterans and the sacrifices they have made to defend our freedom and liberty. Their courage and patriotism to our country is truly awe-inspiring. I have worked alongside and relied upon many of our servicemen and women while promoting national security around the globe. I saw firsthand their dedication to protecting and promoting American values. And I am filled with respect and admiration for their service and for the sacrifices veterans, and their families, have made. **But honoring our veterans and supporting their families goes beyond a single day.** The best way we can honor our veterans is by providing critical housing, employment, and health services for those who need it. And by committing to protecting veterans of all identities and citizenships. We must ensure that our government doesn't just support our active duty heroes and their families, but all veterans who served our nation and the families who support them. **If you send me to Congress, I pledge to work day in and day out to ensure our service members and veterans, here in Texas and around the world, receive the resources they need while defending our country and when they return home.** Please join me today, and every day, in honoring our veterans. In gratitude,
Sri Preston Kulkarni | ******[DONATE NOW](https://act.myngp.com/el/dsa0Yt19-4My3bQbKxdPs4X8H1_c06WV7wNRrLi5HHk=/HDT5ixXaHUQ3G2RXpIHUFWZri-608WpXBbIJqGWBhuM=?refcode=refcode=20191111email&amount=22&recurring=auto)******

name: J.D. Scholten
subject: Today and every day, let's honor our veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scholten4Iowa Campaign Committee
body:
---
Peter --

We are so lucky to live in a land of opportunity, where our freedoms of speech, press, and religion are ensured. As the Iowa state flag proudly states: "Our liberties we prize and our rights we will maintain."

We owe so much of this to the sacrifices of our nation's veterans, including the 4.7 million veterans living in rural America. From the foundation of our country to the present, men and women in uniform have put their lives on the line to protect the freedoms we hold so deeply.

America's veterans deserve recognition, respect, and the benefits and services they've earned. But too often, they face communities that are ill-prepared and underfunded to meet their needs.

Iowa's 4th district has numerous VA facilities, both large and small — yet, it can be over an hour long drive to the closest VA clinic; not to mention the wait times, which can be egregiously long. We must ensure sufficient job training and educational opportunities for veterans as they transition back to civilian life. And we must ensure homeless veterans are given the dignity of a place to live safely and comfortably. Regardless of where veterans choose to live, we have a responsibility to repay our great debt of gratitude for their service by ensuring their health, well-being, and future success.

We can and must do more to ensure that veterans are successfully reintegrated back into civilian life. A "thank you for your service" isn't enough. We must acknowledge the full gravity of asking our friends and neighbors to put their lives on the line for our freedom, safety, and security, and make real strides in upholding our promises to them by investing in their well-being, their care, and their communities.

To the veterans in Iowa's 4th district, and all around the country, know that I have your back — today and every day.

— J.D. Scholten

This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Have questions, concerns, or ideas? Simply reply to this email — we love hearing from supporters! You can also connect with the campaign on social media: Like us on [Facebook](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/11752851/120061569/-579313244?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZDkyNGQ1OS05YzA0LWVhMTEtODI4Yi0yODE4Nzg0ZDZkNjgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6A5j1Q0HS6Jm5Uqi25NoSlpTsz9b7JuQSzBxJNnfp8A=&emci=4b155e5b-0704-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&emdi=dd924d59-9c04-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&ceid=6658013) or follow the campaign on [Twitter](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/11752852/120061575/-1912497674?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZDkyNGQ1OS05YzA0LWVhMTEtODI4Yi0yODE4Nzg0ZDZkNjgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6A5j1Q0HS6Jm5Uqi25NoSlpTsz9b7JuQSzBxJNnfp8A=&emci=4b155e5b-0704-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&emdi=dd924d59-9c04-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&ceid=6658013). Email is one of the most important ways we stay in touch with supporters like you, so we'd hate to see you go; but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](https://email.ngpvan.com/unsubscribeUnique/4b155e5b-0704-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68/dd924d59-9c04-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZDkyNGQ1OS05YzA0LWVhMTEtODI4Yi0yODE4Nzg0ZDZkNjgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6A5j1Q0HS6Jm5Uqi25NoSlpTsz9b7JuQSzBxJNnfp8A=&id=109918160&emailAddress=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) from Scholten4Iowa emails.

name: Christina Finello
subject: Keeping our promises
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Finello for Congress
body:
|
---

| | Hi Peter, As the proud granddaughter of two veterans of the U.S. Navy and Army, I’ve always had a great appreciation for military service \-- we can stand up, vote, and run for office because of all that they fought for. But the fact is: our government doesn’t always deliver on the promises made to our veterans upon their arrival back home. When I worked with Philadelphia Veterans Court, I saw firsthand how many veterans end up in our criminal justice system, struggling with mental health and substance abuse disorders. Through my work with the Department of Behavioral Health, my focus was on connecting veterans to the high-quality care they deserve. We received a grant to enhance the trauma services that veterans received as part of Veterans Court, and my team worked to develop new services specifically designed for veterans, with groups co-led by a clinician and other veterans, and a peer program to connect people going through Veterans Court with other veterans with PTSD who had previously been through the court system. I later joined the board of Alpha Bravo Canine, an organization that places service dogs with veterans and helps train dogs to specifically help veterans with PTSD. We know that service dogs can have a huge impact on improving veterans’ mental health, but because training and providing ongoing food and care for the service dogs is not strictly medical care, the VA has not been able to adequately fund such programs, leaving nonprofit organizations like Alpha Bravo Canine to help veterans cover the costs of their life-saving service dogs. These experiences have made it clear to me that while great work is happening on the local level to support our veterans, the federal government can and should be doing more. We need to have veterans courts in every court system in the country, and we need to fully fund evidence-based care like service animals if we’re going to keep the promises we’ve made to our veterans and service members. That’s why, in Congress, I’ll fight to ensure every veteran has access to the care they need. Thank a veteran for their incredible service and commitment to our country today. I wouldn’t be running if it weren’t for our vets. Thank you, and happy Veterans Day. Christina |
---

name: Mike Thompson
subject: Honor their service
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress
body:
Peter,

Veterans Day is a sacred time to honor those who put their lives on the line to protect our freedoms, those families who support our men and women in uniform, and those who have made the ultimate sacrifice for our nation.

We are forever indebted to our veterans and we must commit to working each day to repay them by providing them the benefits they have earned through their service.

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/jcSrACvjk5C1G4jBhyYEGl-ZwIPbVx1p-n875XCpphg=/DDt0FU0DNDK7ZhI1ZxnxpttXFOmHhwJUXconeXfYCNA=)
So to all our veterans, our men and women currently serving, and their families, we thank you from the bottom of our hearts.

Mike

* * *
_Mike Thompson is a veteran and a longtime public servant. He represents California 's 5th district in Congress, where he fights for our healthcare and a clean environment every day. He cannot continue to do his crucial work without the support of people like you. name: Elisa Cardnell
subject: Honoring veterans every day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Elisa Cardnell for Congress
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/11758848/120117350/1785235803?refcode=header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83Mzg2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MDY1YzgyYy1hZjA0LWVhMTEtODI4Yi0yODE4Nzg0ZDZkNjgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WpI7QwEkN-PoyIldd5_gAaXzHUExGt6pQ3ZZJCLs7YQ=)
Peter,
Happy Veterans Day!
**I want to take this moment to honor every American who has served in our Armed Forces.** They sacrificed blood, sweat, and tears to protect our hard-won freedoms.
During my five years of active duty in the Navy, I was privileged to work shoulder-to-shoulder with men and women who showed me every day what perseverance and integrity looks like. They inspire me to fight hard for our Texas veterans every day.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/11758849/120117352/-1740796449?refcode=1111pic&amount=25&recurring=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83Mzg2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MDY1YzgyYy1hZjA0LWVhMTEtODI4Yi0yODE4Nzg0ZDZkNjgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WpI7QwEkN-PoyIldd5_gAaXzHUExGt6pQ3ZZJCLs7YQ=)
I also want to thank every single military family for the sacrifice they make when their loved ones are deployed - every mom, dad, sibling, and partner deserves recognition for what they do for our country.
It’s critical that we honor our veterans every day - not just today. **We will do that by providing our veterans and their families the resources they have earned when they return home.**
I promise I will never stop fighting for our veterans.
Thanks for all that you do, and happy Veterans Day!
Elisa Cardnell

_Elisa Cardnell is a veteran of the United States Navy. Use of her military rank, job, titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense._

name: Jason Yates
subject: Have you heard this veteran’s story?
body:
| | Today is about hearing their stories and honoring their legacies.
---
| Peter, Few of us know what it’s like to be combated with enemy fire, spend months away from our loved ones, or face grueling physical and emotional challenges while protecting our nation - but a veteran certainly does. Today, we honor the veterans who have sacrificed so much to protect Americans and our freedoms. In 2016 the Department of Veterans Affairs estimated 20.4 million U.S. veterans living in our nation. Every one of these veterans has a unique story, a journey of what led them to serve. They can tell you who has impacted them along the way and who they have lost. Many have scars and wounds they will carry for a lifetime and experiences forever shaping their future. **Do we take the time to hear their stories? Do we even thank our veterans anymore?** **That’s what this day is all about. It’s not about an extra day of vacation or weekend sale at all the stores. Today is set apart to remind Americans to take a moment to listen to our veterans’ stories, to honor them and their sacrifice, and for us to express our sincerest gratitude.** Veterans have millions of remarkable stories of courage and bravery, but we’ll remind you of one brought to light on June 25, 2019, when President Trump presented the Medal of Honor to Staff Sgt. David Bellavia, the first living Iraq War Veteran to ever receive the award. _David Bellavia enlisted in the Army in 1999 as an infantryman. In 2001 he had the choice to leave the service or remain, and in the wake of the terror attacks on 9/11, he felt the call to stay and serve his country._ _In 2004, he deployed to Iraq, and on November 10, 2004, Staff Sgt. Bellavia was a squad leader in support of Operation Phantom Fury in Fallujah, Iraq. Their mission was to clear 12 buildings where insurgents had taken shelter. A Bradley Fighting Vehicle was supposed to provide fire support but failed to do so because of a malfunction._ _The platoon found nothing in the first nine buildings, but as they entered the next compound, they came under heavy machine-gun fire from insurgents, and the soldiers found themselves trapped._ _Staff Sgt. Bellavia provided cover fire for his fellow soldiers to escape. When his unit came under fire a second time, he ran into the room of the house full of tanks and explosives and fought hand-to-hand combat against an insurgent to prevent an explosion._ _Because of Staff Sgt. Bellavia’s selfless courage and willingness to risk his own life, he saved the lives of his comrades._ President Trump said, "America is blessed with the heroes and great people like Staff Sgt. Bellavia, whose intrepid spirit and unwavering resolve defeats our enemies, protects our freedoms and defends our great American flag.” He continued, "David, today we honor your extraordinary courage, we salute your selfless service, and we thank you for carrying on the legacy of American valor that has always made our blessed nation the strongest and mightiest anywhere in the world.” We echo President Trump’s sentiment not just to Sgt. Bellavia, but also to the millions of veterans who have served to protect our country. We hope that as you meet veterans, you will take the time to hear their stories and thank them. Our prayer is that we would not just remember them today, but that we would honor them every time we meet them throughout the year. **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e191111com01&n=1)
---

name: Stephanie Schmid
subject: Honoring our veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stephanie for New Jersey**
body:
Today I'm honoring those who have served our country.
|
---

| | Friends, Service to this country has been a part of my family for generations. My maternal grandfather served on the frontlines in both World War II and the Korean War. Every day--and especially on Veterans Day--I honor and remember all those who have served our country and fought for the freedoms that we have today. As a United States Foreign Service Officer who worked in American embassies around the world, I was proud to work side-by-side with members of the United States military. I witnessed the hard work and sacrifices our service members make in order to serve our country. But too often our service members are not given the support they need when they leave the military and return to civilian life. Our country owes our veterans a great debt and elected officials must work tirelessly every day to ensure that we honor their service by providing the resources and services they need to thrive here at home. I was proud to vote last Tuesday for the state-wide ballot measure that gives New Jersey veterans who live in continuing care communities a $250 annual property-tax deduction--a small step forward in ensuring military members, particularly our elders, receive the financial support they deserve. I'm committed to protecting the rights of veterans and their families everywhere. I'll make sure to continue this fight for our values and our communities here in New Jersey and in Washington. If I am given the privilege of serving my country again, as the Congresswoman from New Jersey's Fourth District, I will prioritize legislation and constituent services that help our veterans. Thank you to all those who have served, and, in Congress, I promise to serve you. -- Stephanie
---
|
---

| | **Paid for by Stephanie for New Jersey**
---
New Jersey native Stephanie Schmid is running for New Jersey's 4th Congressional District. Stephanie has a strong record of serving her community, protecting those most vulnerable, and solving difficult public policy problems. She's running to bring new leadership to Congress at a time when we need our leaders to solve pressing challenges and create opportunities for the people of New Jersey. Stephanie is running a grassroots campaign rooted in New Jersey values. She's not accepting any corporate PAC donations or money from special interests.

Stephanie for New Jersey
P.O. Box 217
Little Silver, NJ 07739

name: MJ Hegar
subject: How we can honor our veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
One of the best ways we can honor our nation's veterans is to reflect on the very real cost of war, and the responsibility we have to both our veterans and the next generation who will put on th

|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890828/7f51f7bc/4bd9f6fb/4ef0b6b3/2532896655/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | Peter --

One of the best ways we can honor our nation's veterans is to reflect on the very real cost of war, and the responsibility we have to both our veterans and the next generation who will put on the uniform.

During my three tours in Afghanistan, I constantly came face-to-face with the realities of war. On my last tour, the Taliban opened fire on my helicopter. I was shot, and my aircraft went down. When rescue helicopters arrived, I strapped myself to the skids and opened fire on the Taliban fighters rushing the crash site below.

I made it out that day, but so many others -- friends of mine, those I served with -- have made the ultimate sacrifice serving our country. **To acknowledge the real, human cost of war, and reserve the deployment of our troops only as a last resort, is to honor the sacrifices that military members and their families make in serving our country.**

Our service members deserve our support as they return from serving our country. I know first-hand what it's like to make the transition back to civilian life, and I will always seek to make good on our promise to our service members by ensuring they receive quality health care, housing assistance, and more.

**I am acutely aware that we have a responsibility to the men and women putting on the uniform today to provide them with the most stable global environment possible as they enter into service.** As your senator, this will be my priority.

Today, and every day, we can honor our veterans by ensuring that the policies we make in Washington reflect the sacrifices they make for us.

\-- MJ

[**DONATE**](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890828/7f51f7bc/4bd9f6fb/4ef0b6b2/2532896655/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjQ5MSJ9/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-veterans-day?refcode=email_20191111vd__491__&amount=10&recurring=true")
---

|

During my three tours in Afghanistan, I constantly came face-to-face with the realities of war. On my last tour, the Taliban opened fire on my helicopter. I was shot, and my aircraft went down. When rescue helicopters arrived, I strapped myself to the skids and opened fire on the Taliban fighters rushing the crash site below. I made it out that day, but so many others -- friends of mine, those I served with -- have made the ultimate sacrifice serving our country. To acknowledge the real, human cost of war, and reserve the deployment of our troops only as a last resort, is to honor the sacrifices that military members and their families make in serving our country. Our service members deserve our support as they return from serving our country. I know first-hand what it's like to make the transition back to civilian life, and I will always seek to make good on our promise to our service members by ensuring they receive quality health care, housing assistance, and more. I am acutely aware that we have a responsibility to the men and women putting on the uniform today to provide them with the most stable global environment possible as they enter into service. As your senator, this will be my priority. Today, and every day, we can honor our veterans by ensuring that the policies we make in Washington reflect the sacrifices they make for us. \-- MJ "" | MJ Hegar was a member of the US Air Force and the Air National Guard. Use of her military rank, job, titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement from the Department of Defense, Air Force, or Air National Guard. MJ for Texas P.O. Box 7156 Round Rock, TX 78683 Contributions or gifts to MJ for Texas are not tax deductible. | | Paid for by MJ for Texas | | If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please "".

name: Cory Booker
subject: here's the plan, team
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
| | |
---
| Hey, did you see Jenna's note below? This is important, friend: Our campaign needs to hit 4% in at least four polls to qualify for the December debate. That's why we launched our Ground Game Fund a few days ago -- to help us reach more people in the early states to introduce more voters and caucus-goers to our campaign. **[Will you chip in to reach the $550,000 we need to raise by 11:59 p.m. on November 15th to fully fund it?](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-decdebate?refcode=em_fr_20191111_CB_20191111-fr-CB-pollingbump-actives_actives_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** Thanks,
Cory _\---FORWARDED MESSAGE---_ Hey there, Jenna here. I wanted to send you a quick note about the December debate. I know we've already written to you about how Cory needs 200,000 individual donors to qualify for that debate. That's not all. **He also needs to hit 4% in four separate polls to secure his spot on the stage -- and we 're not quite there yet.** Now, you may be asking yourself, why hasn't Cory been polling higher? Well, you, _my dad,_ and everyone else I've talked to in the last three days, have been thinking the same thing. Here are a few reasons:
1. Cory didn't enter this race with broad name recognition like Joe Biden or Bernie Sanders.
2. This is the most crowded primary our party has ever seen.
3. And truthfully, our team didn't spend years building out his fundraising list. We had to build some things for scratch when this campaign got started.

**Here 's one thing we know for sure: the more people hear from Cory, the more they like him. [Right now, we're asking everyone to chip in so we can launch ads in the early states and make sure Cory's on the December debate stage.](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-decdebate?refcode=em_fr_20191111_CB_20191111-fr-CB-pollingbump-actives_actives_l2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** Cory's favorability numbers are high -- _some of the highest in this race!_ \-- and we know that he's being actively considered by many voters and caucus-goers. In fact, in many polls, he's being considered by more voters than the top two or three candidates. These ads make a real difference and reach large numbers of voters, many of whom haven't been introduced to Cory yet. **[These ads aren't cheap though. Are you able to send in a few dollars now to help us out?](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-decdebate?refcode=em_fr_20191111_CB_20191111-fr-CB-pollingbump-actives_actives_l3&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** All of us here at HQ are so thankful to have supporters like you. Jenna Jenna Lowenstein
Deputy Campaign Manager
Cory 2020 [](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-decdebate?refcode=em_fr_20191111_CB_20191111-fr-CB-pollingbump-actives_actives_contribbutton&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")
---
| | Paid for by Cory 2020
---

P.O. Box 32009 Newark, NJ 07102 | All content (C) 2019 Cory 2020, All Rights Reserved.
Contributions or gifts to Cory 2020 are not tax deductible.
If you got this message from a friend, [you can add your name now to get email updates directly from Cory 2020.](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0a9b/5fca0d19/798902cf/291c6a38/1975830443/VEsD/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://corybooker.com/join?source=em_emfooter")
This campaign is moving quickly. If you want the most up-to-date information, follow Cory on [Instagram](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0a9b/5fca0d19/798902cf/291c6a07/1975830443/VEsA/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://www.instagram.com/corybooker/"), [Twitter](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0a9b/5fca0d19/798902cf/291c6a06/1975830443/VEsB/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://twitter.com/corybooker"), and [Facebook](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0a9b/5fca0d19/798902cf/291c6a05/1975830443/VEsO/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://www.facebook.com/corybooker").
It's time for leadership that can reignite our spirit of common purpose. That takes all of us. We believe these emails are an important way to stay in touch, but if at any point you'd like to stop getting these updates, you can unsubscribe [here](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0a9b/5fca0d19/798902cf/291c6a04/1975830443/VEsP/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://act.corybooker.com/unsubscribe").
Is this not your preferred email address? You can update your contact info [here](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0a9b/5fca0d19/798902cf/291c6a38/1975830443/VEsHBQ/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://corybooker.com/join?source=em_emfooter").

name: Josh Mahony
subject: Today is Veterans Day
disclaimer_text: paid for by corporations, then contribute any amount to help Josh keep fighting
body:
| [](https://secure.joshuamahony.%3D/?g=x7oqFq0LBwxdSmUxDjMWNpA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mahony-nov?refcode=em191111vets&recurring=auto&refcode2=header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
---
| Friend,

Today is Veterans Day. Today we honor our veterans for everything they have sacrificed and all they've given to our country.

But this Veterans Day is about more than just thanking our vets. It's about showing all of our veterans that we have recommitted ourselves to making sure their interests are represented in Congress. It's about making sure they have access to all the resources they need, like healthcare, benefits, and job opportunities.

If elected, I won't just pay lip service to our veterans -- I will fight for them. I hope you'll stand with me and also commit to helping our country's brave men and women. So today, when you thank a vet, make sure you are also committing to more than just a "thank you for your service."

Thank you,

Josh
---
| | Josh Mahony is a native Arkansan, non-profit leader, and long-time business owner. He's running to replace Tom Cotton and make sure everyday Arkansans have a voice in the Senate. If you want a senator who speaks for you and isn't bought and paid for by corporations, then contribute any amount to help Josh keep fighting. name: Adam Hattersley
subject: This Veterans Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hattersley for Congress
body:
It’s no secret that our country has fallen short on its promise to our veterans.

---
| [****](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/11763031/120156179/1530367080?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjYxMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNmUwYjZkMC1jMDA0LWVhMTEtODI4Yi0yODE4Nzg0ZDZkNjgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AFmG1BHWUaJs3bNW4eikkzmA7UT5kZOcXRgzWsHfpw0=&emci=5bf56b2b-0f04-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&emdi=06e0b6d0-c004-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&ceid=6767893)
Hi Peter,

This weekend I had an amazing experience speaking to hundreds of young students and fellow veterans at the Veterans Day Assembly in Winthrop. I shared with them a few of the invaluable lessons I learned from my time in the military and how those same lessons have shaped me as a public servant.

It’s no secret that our country has fallen short on its promise to our veterans. While I’ve been fortunate in my post-military career -- there are still millions of veterans who are homeless, suffering from life-threatening PTSD, and unable to receive timely critical care.

**It’s estimated that there are at least 17 veterans that die by suicide every day.** It’s a sobering and heartbreaking fact.

I’ve been proud to sponsor legislation in the Florida State House to provide greater access to mental health resources, but our country as a whole needs to do more to prioritize the care for our vets across the country. When I become your congressman — I can promise you I will.

Today, as we honor our veterans, I hope that we will not lose sight of this crisis within the military community. We cannot address a problem we refuse to see.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/11763032/120156180/1530367080?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjYxMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNmUwYjZkMC1jMDA0LWVhMTEtODI4Yi0yODE4Nzg0ZDZkNjgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AFmG1BHWUaJs3bNW4eikkzmA7UT5kZOcXRgzWsHfpw0=&emci=5bf56b2b-0f04-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&emdi=06e0b6d0-c004-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&ceid=6767893)

My greatest thanks to all our men and women in uniform. Thank you for your service.

-Adam

---
| Adam Hattersley is running to represent Florida's 15th Congressional District. To learn more, please visit [hattersleyforcongress.com](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/11763033/120156181/1902165096?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjYxMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNmUwYjZkMC1jMDA0LWVhMTEtODI4Yi0yODE4Nzg0ZDZkNjgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AFmG1BHWUaJs3bNW4eikkzmA7UT5kZOcXRgzWsHfpw0=&emci=5bf56b2b-0f04-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&emdi=06e0b6d0-c004-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&ceid=6767893)

name: Senator Erica Smith
subject: Thank you to all the Veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Erica for US
body:
Dear Supporter,

Today, we honor our armed forces and the courageous men and women who volunteer as warriors to protect our freedom. North Carolina has more than 700,000 Veterans and I promise you that I am committed to serving them and their families to the best of my ability.

Here's to all the brave men and women who stood a post or fought for our democratic freedoms. Let us honor them everyday by working to preserve these very freedoms for every American, regardless of race, creed, nationality, gender or ethnicity. We must, as citizens, all be vigilant guardians of these liberties. In doing so, we honor the sacrifices made on our behalf by our noble Veterans.

Thank you, from the bottom of my heart.

{{Authorized and Paid for by Erica for US .}}

{{Erica For US P.O. Box 1236 Gaston, North Carolina 27832}}

name: Wendy Davis
subject: Fighting for those who fought for us
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wendy Davis for Congress](https://go
body:
|
---
[ ](https://go.wendydavisforcongress.com/page/m/4f361b02/704929dc/1665fdef/39900a8c/53717399/VEsH/?g= "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dav_monthly_template?refcode=dav_logo&amount=25&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wendyforcongress&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A%09%09%09%09%09%09%09&utm_campaign=DAV_EM_FR_191111_VeteransDay_U1_X1&source=DAV_EM_FR_191111_VeteransDay_U1_X1")

| | Friends, Today, we celebrate the brave men and women who defend this nation and honor their tremendous courage and sacrifice. Their tireless sacrifice defines the very meaning of service.- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - Friends, Today, we celebrate the brave men and women who defend this nation and honor their tremendous courage and sacrifice. Their tireless sacrifice defines the very meaning of service. **But our veterans deserve more than just our respect.** While we can never repay the debt we owe them, it is our duty to ensure that we are delivering on our nation's promises to our service men and women -- not just today, but _every_ day. That's why as a member of the Texas Senate Committee on Veterans Affairs and Military Installations, I created a special vehicle for funding veterans' services and worked across the aisle to safeguard critical veterans' programs and expand access to employment opportunities, higher education, and healthcare. **And in Congress, I pledge to continue fighting for those who fought for us.** Whether it's protecting veteran programs from being slashed by politicians in DC, or helping veterans find good paying jobs when they come home, I will work tirelessly for those who have put their lives on the line for our country. **So today, I hope you 'll join me in thanking our veterans for all they have done to preserve our freedom and liberties. **Together, we can ensure that our nation's heroes have every opportunity to succeed when they return home. Much love, Wendy
---

clean_body: | = "" | | Friends, Today, we celebrate the brave men and women who defend this nation and honor their tremendous courage and sacrifice. Their tireless sacrifice defines the very meaning of service.- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - Friends, Today, we celebrate the brave men and women who defend this nation and honor their tremendous courage and sacrifice. Their tireless sacrifice defines the very meaning of service. But our veterans deserve more than just our respect. While we can never repay the debt we owe them, it is our duty to ensure that we are delivering on our nation's promises to our service men and women -- not just today, but every day. That's why as a member of the Texas Senate Committee on Veterans Affairs and Military Installations, I created a special vehicle for funding veterans' services and worked across the aisle to safeguard critical veterans' programs and expand access to employment opportunities, higher education, and healthcare. And in Congress, I pledge to continue fighting for those who fought for us. Whether it's protecting veteran programs from being slashed by politicians in DC, or helping veterans find good paying jobs when they come home, I will work tirelessly for those who have put their lives on the line for our country. So today, I hope you 'll join me in thanking our veterans for all they have done to preserve our freedom and liberties. Together, we can ensure that our nation's heroes have every opportunity to succeed when they return home. Much love, Wendy | = "" | If you'd like to support Wendy Davis for Congress, you can = "" We believe that emails are the best way for our campaign to stay in touch with supporters like you. name: Josh Hawley
subject: A debt we can never repay
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Josh Hawley for Senate
body:
It is my honor to represent so many veterans in MissouriPeter,

More than four hundred thousand. That is how many veterans live in the state of Missouri. Four hundred thousand brave men and women who put their lives on the line to protect us. Four hundred thousand families worried about their loved one but proud that they answered the call to serve. We owe a debt to our veterans that we can never repay. The service and sacrifice they show for us is a constant reminder of what true patriotism means.

It is my honor to represent so many veterans in Missouri, and I promise to never stop working for their needs.

This week, as we're reminded to be grateful for all our blessings, I am incredibly thankful that we are protected by men and women such as these and for the freedoms they continue to defend. I hope you'll join me in thanking them for their service, and praying for their safe future.

Happy Veterans Day and God bless America,

Josh Hawley

Paid for by Josh Hawley for Senate

name: Michael Bennet
subject: To those who served, thank you.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for America
body:
| |
---
| Dear Friend -

Today, we pay tribute to the men and women who served our nation in uniform. They answered every call and made every sacrifice, in the name of protecting and defending our country and Constitution.

They did this for the American ideal -- that a strong democracy is the greatest force for good in the world.

Although we can never fully repay the debt we owe them, we can ensure they come home to a grateful nation that serves them and their families just as they served us.

As president, I will fight to ensure that our veterans receive the respect, services, and support they have earned and deserve.

**Today and every day: thank you.**

Michael
---

| | Paid for by Bennet for America
---

name: Doyle Canning
subject: End the Forever War
body:
That photo was taken twelve years ago, and yet these occupations continue. | | [ ](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/4QA/ni0YAA/t.2wm/O1mbmaVkQmGd9I4r4zw6aQ/h0/EITbMdFK8sEmH288ys-2B-2Fh8ph-2FrB9A6-2BM0i5qCb2xsCyiK7xXkJftbeIVM7HbMWah-2F-2FTUy7T-2FpE-2Ban7TzilxtVHMJk6Ko3XMudiK22ctjhsmRptTnUeLyseIwv7dBH718etZFiMPcVW-2BWF4BDpnfwTSLL7OHGQD8GnTVp1ia-2FrL8XIL3VJ7cK-2B3NQc7byO966JmsWmcCoMA1Z9OKkF4p1bFLYMBsqAZ7x0Apz1Yk9WQrPe8Yt9k-2FxVPud9hBSVByU/Jn7V)
---
Friend, Our country's military has been in a permanent state of ever-expanding conflict for over seventeen years. Veterans call it the "Forever War." On Veterans Day, we must renew our commitment to end it. Today I have signed onto [this pledge](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/4QA/ni0YAA/t.2wm/O1mbmaVkQmGd9I4r4zw6aQ/h1/0mNE3yq0WoypbYLFO5Zal3mjXW9d3io-2Fk9tn8xxAaRPLc2XxMYREcrwITmwFnkeowejXobzmkqnkDZ1LQAnxxmnfH5DU4vxkZ9TtiSXOQjdmS3xyNtQm8KF82BAZ9FWrm5XLXWSD7H60QVO6Q3eSri71gDZJu2bcYqQ4r4e-2B3V2rVIJL52UEMLbbyr-2FDJj5hLrzk0pq1xxwMGj6Zj4jWzxbrwKFv-2BU3i01yRKk55-2BKPXFCNcl5Zp-2Fq32F6Cn3Wu8/0twU) to end the Forever War and stand with Veterans and civilians working for change. Please join me. Back in 2005, I began my work with the newly forming grassroots organization Iraq Veterans Against the War (IVAW), to end the war in Iraq, and to fight for those who came home. Over the following decade I supported IVAW in building their movement. One of the greatest honors of my life was assisting IVAW in convening [Winter Soldier: Iraq and Afghanistan](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/4QA/ni0YAA/t.2wm/O1mbmaVkQmGd9I4r4zw6aQ/h2/Pdu5VCejBHu-2FJVvN6TQbOb2AjMyfFP9mSF8yDEpFiSh-2FlcEVy6jo3TqJxcrhrAXHd-2FVzOp7QqYRUrLc8lXogJDxykFbCyLNa3R-2B-2FpuzPRHrmrK5hWy8nBMmeyXLu-2FlSW84nM8RyO2xqITcnqkshPMZQVevR0LjXiG-2BvxR879bxlH0VAUy5bM71f0Y3L25x-2B-2BSJUPovTGUo0fA27zPc7hiVkZxEPput7aDdyUcPDCogA-3D/GlBJ). Over three days of searing testimony, veterans spoke their ground truth about the occupations, and raised their voices to end them. Here is a photo from one of the first planning meetings we held for Winter Solider, on Veterans Day weekend in 2007:
---
|
---
That photo was taken twelve years ago, and yet these occupations continue. Since the invasion, [7,027](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/4QA/ni0YAA/t.2wm/O1mbmaVkQmGd9I4r4zw6aQ/h3/BqON3S6ye953Dgj8nEq53jevW7NfqWM1YL7-2BGXRVgqoPRL29b2jtNb0n9ymXsW-2F50jhZJnoAsZL-2Bi276sdrE6tpPakegaJNz9by0hWSWUkvUVbbhsTzaeUvxEsQ-2FCBNoWaluT4Of-2BLDlTHtN6HPugAZCzzCzis-2BqbR4SuFZ8MFZItWmwvpDrQoM-2B68XeYI5rx9DlcwSNZ9Ckl96KWkgQHIZAYCnH285SvGDOO6ZacpkdiE0wNZtAxdoX4jiiuLbZ/ac1Q) American service members have lost their lives, almost [60,000](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/4QA/ni0YAA/t.2wm/O1mbmaVkQmGd9I4r4zw6aQ/h4/yPElbhaudbjo02tRN8e-2BUh6WuGyf60hVWep-2F6L9s9othKkdgGJmYW4D22GO7cbJKQwpM1vGYecsYaCkYk0Mvk6MqWVAJtFEssLBGF5LUMRFixIVBvA30I-2FB1sXVQuv7ZSTk6LB9dCdeg41OKQS25ALub2PkaSpGUAFSAlUa0i3xP58g1dLEgUgYf8nfnSTzg-2FXe6aIybzjOAvDDfCckiVfMjqs-2BnzhWl1JlF3Tjymc0C4UmtZyU64Hr-2BYkK2txh2bWpExg7rs2lUuatFz4UwYQ-3D-3D/rdMG) have been injured, and countless more live every day with the invisible wounds of war. Meanwhile, the military industrial complex has only grown, while oil companies enjoy the spoils of our service members' sacrifices. It is time to recognize that the burden of these wars have been carried by our military personnel and their families, who have endured repeated deployments year after year. Today we honor them, remember them, and vow to fight with them for real change. I will always stand with veterans, and I will never take a dime from defense contractors or war profiteers. In 2020, it is time to take a stand to end the forever wars. Onward, Doyle P.S. We are convening a roundtable of veterans in the district, looking for input on our veterans and foreign policy platforms and to grow our Veterans for Canning committee. If you are a veteran, service member, or member of a military family, I would love your perspective and suggestions. Please contact [kelly@canningforcongress.com](mailto:kelly@canningforcongress.com) if you want to get involved.
---

clean_body: That photo was taken twelve years ago, and yet these occupations continue. | | Friend, Our country's military has been in a permanent state of ever-expanding conflict for over seventeen years. Veterans call it the "Forever War." On Veterans Day, we must renew our commitment to end it. Today I have signed onto to end the Forever War and stand with Veterans and civilians working for change. Please join me. Back in 2005, I began my work with the newly forming grassroots organization Iraq Veterans Against the War (IVAW), to end the war in Iraq, and to fight for those who came home. Over the following decade I supported IVAW in building their movement. One of the greatest honors of my life was assisting IVAW in convening . Over three days of searing testimony, veterans spoke their ground truth about the occupations, and raised their voices to end them. name: Gary Peters
subject: Veterans Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY PETERS FOR MICHIGAN
body:
Today we honor those brave men and women who donned the uniform in service to our nation. They risked everything in defense of our country and then came home to continue building a stronger America after the end of their military service.
[](https://secure.petersformichigan.com/page/m/7b6daa53/5721a3ed/711c125d/efa8c4e/1244214042/VEsH/?g=SkKTSlNw52hcsC2e00JiNrA "https://petersformichigan.com/")
[](https://secure.petersformichigan.com/page/m/7b6daa53/5721a3ed/711c125d/efa8c4e/1244214042/VEsE/?g=SkKTSlNw52hcsC2e00JiNrA "https://petersformichigan.com/")
Today we honor those brave men and women who donned the uniform in service to our nation. They risked everything in defense of our country and then came home to continue building a stronger America after the end of their military service.
We owe our veterans a deep debt of gratitude, which is why we must always ensure that they have access to the benefits they deserve and have earned. That's why I am fighting for them every day in the U.S. Senate.
On Veterans Day, I think of the men and women I served with in the U.S. Navy Reserve and their dedication to country. I think of my father who served in Europe during World War II, and how grateful I am for his bravery and that of his generation.
Most of all, I think of the men and women I have met who are veterans of our most recent conflicts, and how they instill in me continued confidence that America remains the greatest and freest nation in the world thanks to their service and sacrifice. Let us never fail to honor them, for they have truly honored us with their heroism.
On this Veterans Day, please join me in saluting the service and sacrifice of America's veterans.
Gary
