name: Mike Franken
subject: What it means to be a veteran
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Franken for Iowa
body:
I expect most of you are enjoying this Veterans Day weekend. But if I may ask one thing of you -- take a few minutes today to reflect on how a veteran changed your life.
|
---

| | Dear friends and fellow veterans, I expect most of you are enjoying this Veterans Day weekend. But if I may ask one thing of you -- take a few minutes today to reflect on how a veteran changed your life. It could be a fellow service member, a commanding officer, a friend, maybe a grandparent, parent, son or daughter... Think how their service influenced you and those you know. Here are my thoughts. I had one grandparent gassed in World War I; he lived with his damaged heart and lungs for decades afterward and made a successful life for his family. His brother, also wounded, never recovered. My father survived a ship sinking in the Battle of Savo Island during WW II and had to swim through the night with burns and shrapnel injuries until rescued the next day. He never talked about it. My brother and a host of brothers-in-law served as well, each etching their own story -- during Vietnam, mostly -- in their family lives and for the next generation. My nephews are continuing the legacy of service above self, while other family members choose to serve the public good in other, equally meaningful ways. The short answer is that service to others is a good-news story. My personal story has a byline. The hundreds of Air Force, Army, Marine Corps, Navy, Coast Guard, government civilians, aid workers, and contractors who so ably supported my commands and me through the years deserve my humble appreciation. Most worked and sacrificed without ever asking anything for themselves -- missing weddings, birthdays, holidays, and even funerals -- just so that the command did what was asked of it. I salute every one of those people, today and every day. So please, reflect on what it means to serve, and find your little way to continue to give back to our great society. Respectfully,
Mike Franken
---
|
---

| | Paid for by Franken for Iowa
---

name: =?utf-8?Q?Juli=C3=A1n_Castro?=
subject: This Veteran's Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Julian for the Future
body:
| | |
---
| | [](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fc81c/3eb3e2fc/56f56dd7/744d9d7d/3746476174/VEsH/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://www.julianforthefuture.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=20191111_em_vetday&source=20191111_em_vetday")
---
| | As the former mayor of "Military City, U.S.A," I'm writing today to share my deepest appreciation for all of our nation's veterans. Sixty-five years ago, we established this holiday to celebrate, thank, and honor members of the Armed Forces that have defended our nation's freedom and protected our country's future. To America's veterans, you have our utmost respect and gratitude for your service. To the families who have lost service members, we honor your sacrifices. **We owe veterans not only a debt of gratitude, but a lifetime of support.** During the Obama administration, through a bipartisan, multi-agency effort, we reduced veteran homelessness by nearly 50 percent. Our work is not yet complete, so as a candidate I've set an ambitious goal of eliminating veteran homelessness by the end of my first term as president. I've had the opportunity to serve and work with veterans who represent the very best of the United States. And today, we cannot forget the immigrants who served our country only to be deported. As president, I will work to bring these veterans back, reunite them with their families, and create an immigration system based on common sense and compassion. We are forever grateful for those who wear and have worn the stars and stripes on their shoulders. With gratitude, Julian
---

No one like Julian Castro has ever been in the Oval Office. His grandmother immigrated here from Mexico when she was just 7 years old. She never got past the 4th grade, but she provided for her family by working as a maid and a housekeeper.

Now, her grandson is running for President of the United States. Against all odds, grassroots Democrats like YOU helped him qualify for the Democratic debates and sent him to the front of the pack of Democratic candidates.

We know we send you a lot of emails, but that's because we're relying on grassroots supporters to overcome our next big challenge:

Julian must be able to compete and build a movement that lasts for the long haul, and he's not taking a cent of corporate PAC money to do it. Will you be a part of this historic campaign?

name: When Democrats Turn Out
subject: Betsy DeVos is an awful Secretary of Education and needs to resign
disclaimer_text: Paid for by When Democrats Turn Out PAC
body:
|
---

| | Hello Peter, Betsy DeVos has betrayed the core tenets of the Department of Education as Trump's Secretary \-- she has slashed funding to critical programs, she has withdrawn protections for LGBT students and exposed them to harassment and expulsion, and she has destroyed Title IX protections for survivors of sexual assault. [**She has to go. The damage she has done to our education system cannot continue any longer. name: Team Stanton
subject: this would be a terrible step backwards
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stanton for Congress
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/3PU4m315RScXREIDNOCrWkxa8fe3D2kj_MTu_8Cn7Ow=/r6ubiTakysDV3zf3dfClDf2OpuPiPmVjQYG2dz8G9C0=)
Under President Obama's leadership, our nation took a huge leap forward in tackling the global threat of climate change when we joined the Paris Climate Agreement.

Now, President Trump is trying to erase this legacy of the Obama Administration by withdrawing our country from this landmark international accord.

**This would be a terrible step backwards for the global standing of our country and for the climate future of our planet. **

[**Will you add your name to this petition to join Greg in calling on the Trump Administration to stay in the Paris Climate Agreement?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/3PU4m315RScXREIDNOCrWkxa8fe3D2kj_MTu_8Cn7Ow=/r6ubiTakysDV3zf3dfClDf2OpuPiPmVjQYG2dz8G9C0=)

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/3PU4m315RScXREIDNOCrWkxa8fe3D2kj_MTu_8Cn7Ow=/r6ubiTakysDV3zf3dfClDf2OpuPiPmVjQYG2dz8G9C0=)

**Look, for far too long, partisan differences have hindered our ability to tackle the climate crisis.**

But as we have seen from hurricanes in the Caribbean, wildfires in California, and drought here in Arizona -- we are running out of time to come together to stop climate change.

[**Tell Trump -- Commit to Combatting Climate Change by Staying in the Paris Climate Agreement >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/3PU4m315RScXREIDNOCrWkxa8fe3D2kj_MTu_8Cn7Ow=/r6ubiTakysDV3zf3dfClDf2OpuPiPmVjQYG2dz8G9C0=)

Thank you for signing on,

\-- _Team Stanton _

name: Tulsi Gabbard
subject: Our Veterans Deserve More
body:
| | | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/11767055/120213527/1624370312?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS83MjQxNiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YWU5ODQ5Zi1lMTA0LWVhMTEtODI4Yi0yODE4Nzg0ZDZkNjgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qsHYvuPUYFrr0KPPvII6mpBFh31WFwL_wIvlk7qzTgI=&emci=bef765df-d904-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&emdi=5ae9849f-e104-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&ceid=661311)
---
| Today, I stand with Americans around the country in honoring my fellow veterans, those still with us and those who made the ultimate sacrifice defending the country we love. Throughout my 16 years as a soldier, including two deployments to the Middle East, I have witnessed the very best of humanity — even during the darkest of moments — in the patriots I’ve had the privilege of serving with. **Today, they deserve more.** On this day we hear platitudes and words of thanks from politicians. But too often, this rhetoric is hollow — and the self-serving politicians who’d salute a veteran or march in a parade end up perpetuating the very same policies that have caused so much unnecessary suffering. **This hypocrisy must end.** **The best way we can honor my brothers and sisters in uniform is by only sending them on missions worthy of their great sacrifice. We can honor them by caring for them when they come home.** Making sure they get benefits they’ve earned. By doing all we can to prevent more of our veterans from taking their own lives by suicide. Empowering them to continue to serve when they return to civilian life. We honor them by reinvesting the trillions of dollars we’ve wasted on deadly regime change wars, the new Cold War and nuclear arms race, back in the pockets of the American people. **As president, I will show our veterans the respect they deserve with actions that truly honor all those who risk their lives for the safety, security and freedom of all Americans.** [Do you know a veteran who needs to hear this message today? Watch this video, and then help me get the message out.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/11767056/120213528/889249402?sourceid=1014165&ms=em191111&utm_source=newsletter&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=em191111&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS83MjQxNiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YWU5ODQ5Zi1lMTA0LWVhMTEtODI4Yi0yODE4Nzg0ZDZkNjgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qsHYvuPUYFrr0KPPvII6mpBFh31WFwL_wIvlk7qzTgI=&emci=bef765df-d904-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&emdi=5ae9849f-e104-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&ceid=661311) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/11767057/120213529/889249402?sourceid=1014165&ms=em191111&utm_source=newsletter&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=em191111&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS83MjQxNiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YWU5ODQ5Zi1lMTA0LWVhMTEtODI4Yi0yODE4Nzg0ZDZkNjgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qsHYvuPUYFrr0KPPvII6mpBFh31WFwL_wIvlk7qzTgI=&emci=bef765df-d904-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&emdi=5ae9849f-e104-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&ceid=661311) Politicians in both parties have failed to truly honor our sacrifices. Serving in a medical unit in Iraq, I saw firsthand the high costs of interventionist policies launched in the naive and wrongheaded belief that we should police the world and impose our will on foreign societies through brute force. Our soldiers signed up to protect America, not pillage other nations. That’s why a centerpiece of my campaign for president has been calling for the precious resources we have squandered on ill-fated, destructive military interventions to be redirected back home to improve the quality of life for our veterans and all Americans. **Imagine if the trillions of dollars wasted on regime change wars since 9/11 had been left in the pockets of the American people,** or invested in health care, infrastructure, education and protecting our environment. The missed opportunities are incalculable. We need a commander-in-chief with the strength and independence to resist the pressures of interventionists and those who profit off the backs of our men and women in uniform. One with a record of standing up to the military industrial complex, one who will never send our troops into harm’s way unless it is absolutely necessary and who will take care of those who return home. I enlisted after the attacks on 9/11 out of a deep conviction that **service above self is our highest calling.** As president, I will honor that calling with actions deserving the sacrifice every soldier and veteran makes for our great nation. Today we honor all of you who serve, who have sacrificed, and who continue to fight for our freedoms. TULSI | [WATCH](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/11767058/120213530/889249402?sourceid=1014165&ms=em191111&utm_source=newsletter&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=em191111&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS83MjQxNiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YWU5ODQ5Zi1lMTA0LWVhMTEtODI4Yi0yODE4Nzg0ZDZkNjgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qsHYvuPUYFrr0KPPvII6mpBFh31WFwL_wIvlk7qzTgI=&emci=bef765df-d904-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&emdi=5ae9849f-e104-ea11-828b-2818784d6d68&ceid=661311 "Button")

name: James Mackler
subject: Today has personal significance for me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by James Mackler for U
body:
What happened sixteen years ago:
|
---
[ ](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa232/18d6a294/4cec1110/26d63a6c/4012420602/VEsH/?g=HpMoNK0LBwxckIwjLS9nKqA "https://www.jamesmackler.com/")

| | On Veterans Day, we thank the brave men and women who defend our country and keep us safe. Today also has added personal significance for me. With our nation in crisis after 9/11, I knew I had to do something. So at 30 years old, I closed my law practice, and on Veterans Day sixteen years ago, I left home to report to a Military Entrance Processing Station and joined the U.S. Army the next day. In the years since, I've spent time on Veterans Day reflecting on my service, the service of others, and the state of our nation. In recent years, I've been deeply troubled by the issues we face. Powers, both foreign and domestic, threaten to divide us, while Washington remains so broken that our leaders can't or won't work together to address them. I decided to run for U.S. Senate because I felt a lot like I did more than a decade ago: our nation is in a crisis and I had to do something. I'm running to fight for the American values that my brothers and sisters-in-arms served and sacrificed to protect and defend. Please join me today and thank our servicemen and women for the brave sacrifices they've made for our country. Thank you, James
---

|
---
| | Paid for by James Mackler for U.S. Senate
---
James Mackler is a member of the Tennessee National Guard and a former member of the U.S. Army. name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Add your name if you agree: It's time for meaningful action and policies that support our military and military families both during and after service -- not just on Veterans Day, but every day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Our service members, veterans, and their families are smart, tough, and resourceful -- they will accomplish any mission we ask of them, whatever the cost. It's time for a government that fights as... | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285288b4/45c30650/1048e5555/66f450e0/2355616720/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
---
Today, we gather across the country to thank our military personnel past and present. I'm grateful for my three older brothers, who all served.

Too often, the day-to-day sacrifices of service members, veterans, and military families go unseen and unremarked. It's time for meaningful action and policies that support our military and military families both during and after service -- not just on Veterans Day, but every day.

We can honor our veterans by ending these endless wars, reining in our bloated defense budget and reducing the influence of defense contractors at the Pentagon, and bringing our troops home responsibly -- and then providing our veterans with the benefits they've earned.

That's why last week, I released my plan to support our service members, veterans, and military families -- including protecting earned benefits, prioritizing family readiness, tackling military sexual assault, cutting veterans' suicide in half in my first term, ensuring equal treatment for all who serve, easing the transition for veterans, and creating a 21st century VA health care system.

**Our service members, veterans, and their families are smart, tough, and resourceful -- they will accomplish any mission we ask of them, whatever the cost. It's time for a government that fights as hard for them as they do for us.**

**[Add your name if you agree we need to do a lot more to care for our service members, veterans, and military families, and support our plan to help make it happen.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285288b4/45c30650/1048e5555/66f450e3/2355616720/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTUwzMlBfUkxUX01JTF9FXzIwMTkxMTExX1YxUzNfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-service-members?source=EML32P_RLT_MIL_E_20191111_V1S3_P&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

**-> We start by protecting the earned benefits for those who serve, because it's about honoring the commitment of those who choose to serve with commitments of our own.** As president, I will propose pay raises at or above the Employment Cost Index to ensure that compensation remains competitive with the civilian sector and that it reflects the unique demands of military life.

We'll also empower our military students through my plan for affordable higher education that makes two- and four-year public college free, and cancels student loan debt up to $50,000 for 42 million Americans. This will provide relief for thousands of military families burdened with higher education expenses beyond what is covered by the GI Bill, and ensure that all of our veterans and their families have the chance to get essential job training and degrees without taking on a dime of student loan debt.

As president, I'll also work with Congress to give the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau new tools and additional authority to enforce the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act to protect military families from financial scams.

**-> Next, my plan prioritizes military family readiness.** Military families form the backbone of our armed forces, and we know that they face special challenges, like regular moves from assignment to assignment and the anxiety of a loved one's deployment.

From increasing spousal employment; to providing universal child care, investing again in DOD child care and modernizing schools on base; to supporting wounded warriors and their families; to providing safe and affordable military housing, my plan ensures military readiness by supporting and uplifting military families.

**-> My plan will also put service members and veterans first.** Nearly two decades of combat has put significant stress on the force, and this will continue to manifest itself long after combat operations are over. Our first priority must be the care and safety of those who serve or have served in uniform. That means tackling military sexual assault, pursuing a suite of concrete policies to cut veteran suicides in half within my first term, treating the opioid and addiction crisis, and addressing the "invisible wounds" of war.

**-> We'll also ensure equal treatment for all who serve.** When we discriminate or treat classes of service members as less worthy than their peers, we fail to honor the diversity of our force, and we harm our ability to recruit the strongest possible future force. Minority communities in the military -- particularly LGBTQ+, women, Black, and Latinx service members -- are significantly under-represented in the leadership ranks. My plan pays special attention to these communities to ensure that we are protecting and honoring everyone who volunteers to serve.

**-> My plan will also ease the transition for veterans.** Many new veterans struggle to find a sense of purpose or connection in new civilian careers and communities. While the Department of Defense has improved its transition counseling in recent years, we can do more to prepare service members to return to civilian life. My plan ensures a "warm hand-off" between the DoD and the VA, eliminates the benefits backlog, clears "bad paper" discharges, helps transitioning service members find good jobs, and works to end veterans' homelessness.

**-> Finally, my plan creates a 21st Century VA Health Care System.** In recent years, attacks on the VA have intensified as Republicans have pressed to privatize large chunks of VA service. A Warren administration will be clear-eyed about leadership challenges at VA. We will hold accountable leaders who fail to put veterans first or misuse resources, and we will empower whistleblowers who report wrongdoing to address their concerns and protect them from retaliation. While community care is appropriate where specialists are unavailable or geographically inaccessible, let me be clear: a Warren administration will invest in the VA, not further dismantle it. We will not cut the high-quality, evidence-based, culturally competent programs that our veterans rely on.

**It 's time for a government that lives up to its values by keeping its promises to those who served. [Will you support my plan for service members, veterans, and military families by adding your name?](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285288b4/45c30650/1048e5555/66f450e3/2355616720/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTUwzMlBfUkxUX01JTF9FXzIwMTkxMTExX1YxUzNfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-service-members?source=EML32P_RLT_MIL_E_20191111_V1S3_P&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

P.S. Are you a current or retired service member? **[Click here to join our Veterans for Warren community group on the All In for Warren platform](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285288b4/45c30650/1048e5555/66f450e2/2355616720/VEsD/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://community.elizabethwarren.com/registrations/groups/33087")** -- meet fellow Veterans for Warren, connect with the campaign's Veterans & Military Families Outreach Director, and discuss my new plan to support our service members, veterans, and military families.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Marc=20Flores?=
subject: Meet our new Field Director!
body:
** Good afternoon Peter,
------------------------------------------------------------

We wanted to let you know about the latest addition to our team! In early October, we hired Ramiro Ojeda as our official Field Director.

Ramiro has had extensive experience working for representatives in public service, and has worked in canvassing on a number of elections. Having graduated from University of Houston with a B.S. in Political Science and a B.A. in Philosophy, we are excited to welcome him onto the campaign!

"What I enjoy most about working on the campaign is that we go all over the district and we learn so much from everyone that we meet on the campaign trail. Some of the people we've met have so much to say, and you just constantly learn not just from them but about their neighborhood and how much Houston has grown."

-Ramiro Ojeda
Field Director, Marc Flores for Congress

** We want you to meet Ramiro!
Come out to either of the following events to get to know our new Field Director:
------------------------------------------------------------
* Phone bank (https://www.mobilize.us/marcfloresforcongress/event/152383/?utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=72f99027e5-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2019_11_11_06_44&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-72f99027e5-32267927) with us Wednesday night at campaign HQ, at 5:30 PM
* Knock on doors (https://www.mobilize.us/marcfloresforcongress/event/152435/?utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=72f99027e5-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2019_11_11_06_44&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-72f99027e5-32267927) with us this Saturday at 9 AM

For more information on either event, or to sign up for them, click here (https://www.mobilize.us/marcfloresforcongress/?utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=72f99027e5-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2019_11_11_06_44&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-72f99027e5-32267927) !

clean_body: Good afternoon Peter, We wanted to let you know about the latest addition to our team! In early October, we hired Ramiro Ojeda as our official Field Director. Ramiro has had extensive experience working for representatives in public service, and has worked in canvassing on a number of elections. Having graduated from University of Houston with a B.S. in Political Science and a B.A. in Philosophy, we are excited to welcome him onto the campaign! "What I enjoy most about working on the campaign is that we go all over the district and we learn so much from everyone that we meet on the campaign trail. Some of the people we've met have so much to say, and you just constantly learn not just from them but about their neighborhood and how much Houston has grown." -Ramiro Ojeda Field Director, Marc Flores for Congress We want you to meet Ramiro! Come out to either of the following events to get to know our new Field Director: Phone bank () with us Wednesday night at campaign HQ, at 5:30 PM Knock on doors () with us this Saturday at 9 AM For more information on either event, or to sign up for them, click here () ! Find Out More! () ============================================================ Facebook () Twitter () Link () Website () Copyright © 2019 Marc Flores for Congress, All rights reserved.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Heidi=20Sloan=20for=20Congress?=
subject: Welcome to our movement!
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://us3.campaign-archive.com/?e=c169cb0a8e&u=11eb676804bfa0f7eac2ca97a&id=ff016686f5)
---
| |
---
| |
# It's time to fight.
Building a working-class movement won't be easy. The labor we have sacrificed to line the pockets of the wealthy is no longer acceptable. This country has a deep history of working-class struggle, and we must continue to add to that history to earn the rights we are long-past due.

Our lives are shaped around survival, piecing pennies together to ensure that our families are safe and healthy.

It doesn't have to be this way.

We work everyday and while the wealthy get rich off our work we only get to take a few crumbs home. People like Roger Williams, who benefit from our suffering, while sitting congressional seats that will make them more rich and powerful year after year.

It doesn't have to be this way.

Heidi is building a movement of working-class people to reclaim our power, starting with this congressional seat. To do that, we have to get our message to not hundreds, not thousands, but hundreds of thousands of working-class Texans.

We're asking you to help spread that message.

We're going to talk to those people where they are, and that means reaching out in many ways, easy and hard. Sharing our posts on social media so that people can see the story of this campaign, text or phone banking to make sure we can talk to people in the most remote reaches of this district, knocking doors alongside dozens of other working-class organizers ready for change, or even just throwing in a few dollars so that the people block walking in the Texas sun can stay supplied with fliers and forms.

There's a lot of ways for us to be in this fight, but we all have to be in it together.
---
|
---
| One Fight. All In.
---

name: Margaret Good
subject: We honor our veterans
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/78201/images/Good-Logo-2019-FINAL%20%283%29.jpg]

Friend,

Today is a day to honor and thank everyone who has served our country and sacrificed so much for this nation— especially those veterans who call Florida’s 16th district home.

As someone that comes from a family of service myself, I understand the personal price veterans and their families pay in service to our country. My uncle died in Vietnam, my grandfather fought in World War II, and my great-grandfather fought in WorId War I. It’s because of their sacrifice and the sacrifice of millions of veterans that we enjoy our freedom today. I promise that every day I will continue to dedicate myself to ensuring that our vets have the resources they need to be safe and successful.

Our vets shouldn’t leave service and have to worry about paying for healthcare or where their next meal is coming from. We owe it to them to spend every day — not just Veterans Day — trying to make life better for those who have given part of their life in service to us.

To everyone who has or is currently serving: thank you, and please know that my priority is to make sure that you receive the benefits you have earned — both while in service, and after you hang up your uniform.

Thank you,

Margaret

Margaret Good for Congress
PO Box 5073
Sarasota , FL 34277
United States

name: Laura Loomer
subject: This is who protects you . . .
body:
With fear in their eyes, the retreating French warned the Marines to retreat, that they would all be killed.
| | | [](http://em.lauraloomerforcongress.com/ga/click/2-561017072-4395-353215-822467-4714287-330f718eb7-fa46a91cce)
Patriot,

On August 14, 1900, a young Marine was ordered to secure a position during the Boxer Rebellion in Peking, China.

The Marine, Dan Daly, a former paperboy from Long Island, stood at 5'6" and weighed just 134 pounds. He wasn't an imposing figure at all.

But Dan Daly had the fighting spirit of a giant.

He was left alone with a simple order to hold a crumbling fort wall overnight.

As night fell, hordes of Boxers attacked Daly’s position within the intent to overrun and invade the military base. With just one man defending the position, the enemy thought the task would be easy.

But they underestimated the fighting spirit of one American from the streets of New York.

Over the night, wave after wave of Chinese advanced on Daly.

Despite being alone, Daly honored his order. He didn’t retreat, he didn’t hide, and he certainly didn’t back down, Dan Daly fought!

When the sun rose, Daly looked down from his position to see the bodies of 200 Boxers whose last decision on earth was to challenge a United States Marine.

Seventeen years later, during World War 1, a string of defeated French soldiers were limping away from a wheat field in France.

The field surrounded what was known as Belleau Wood, a rock-filled forest entrenched with 5 German Divisions and their machine guns.

The French had attempted to take the forest but were raked by German guns when they stepped into the open field.

As the French were marching out, the Marines were marching in.

With fear in their eyes, the retreating French warned the Marines to retreat, that they would all be killed.

When a French soldier told Marine Captain Lloyd Williams and his men to turn around, he replied, “Retreat? Hell, we just got here!”

The Marines advanced through the waist-high wheat field, and among them was Sgt. Dan Daly, by then a recipient of TWO Medals of Honor with nothing more to prove.

The first wave of Marines were slaughtered. Still, they continued with Daly motivating his men with the famous cry, "Come on, you sons of bitches, do you want to live forever?"

They fought their way into the woods, and for the next 19 days, the Marines fought the Germans without relief.

Exhausted and low on food and ammunition, the Marines ran through the woods, bare-chested with fixed-bayonets. As they fought, they growled and barked like dogs from hell to terrify and drive out the disciplined German soldiers.

And on June 26, 1917, an American Major radioed in to report, “Woods now U.S. Marine Corps entirely.”

Following the battle, the French renamed Belleau Wood, “Wood of the Marine Brigade.”

The Germans also awarded the Marines a name after the battle . . . “Teufel Hunden" or as Marines are known today, "Devil Dogs."

And the paperboy from Long Island? Dan Daly survived the battle. He was recommended for a third Medal of Honor for his displays of bravery in France, but officials felt it was too much to give one man three medals.

Dan Daly exemplifies the American fighting spirit that flows through every soldier, sailor, airman, and Marine.

That fighting spirit is the reason why you and I can sleep well at night, knowing that we’re not just safe, but our enemies lay awake, wide-eyed, fearing the growl of our Devil Dogs.

And may God have mercy on any enemy that crosses American soil, as 18.8 million veterans are alive today. Please thank one of them today.

Happy Veteran’s Day,

Laura Loomer

P.S. If you served in our Armed Forces, thank you so much for your sacrifice for us. You have my eternal respect and gratitude.

LAKE WORTH, FL. 33460

## It’s the quarterfinals - don’t forget to vote tonight!
---
| | Happy Veterans Day and thank you to all those who serve. We have the greatest country in the world because we have the greatest military in the world.
Tonight on Dancing with the Stars it’s the quarterfinals and I will have 8 representatives from Yellow Ribbon Fund join me for the show in honor of Veterans Day. Every week I have had the honor of having veterans and their caregivers as my guests on set and to have them here on Veteran’s Day is especially meaningful.

I’m honored to work with two great veterans organizations, The Independence Fund and The Yellow Ribbon Fund. The Independence Fund who provides tracked wheel chairs to veterans with mobility issues to help give them back some of their own independence. The Yellow Ribbon Fund provides critical support to injured service members and their caregivers.

In addition, all the proceeds from “campaign merchandise” sold on [SpicerArnold.com](https://seanspicer.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d009170fb4604b5974b5521bd&id=864ca61044&e=f6564bbe3b) goes to benefit both of these organizations.
---
| | | **_How to vote:_**
You can submit 20 votes total for Sean and Jenna. Please make sure to cast all 20.

You can submit 10 votes by texting SEAN to 21523. Each text is one vote, you have to send SEAN 10 times to submit all 10 votes.

You can submit 10 additional votes online at [ABC.com](https://seanspicer.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d009170fb4604b5974b5521bd&id=4b48532358&e=f6564bbe3b). You need to start by making an account [here](https://seanspicer.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d009170fb4604b5974b5521bd&id=e34fdebc9a&e=f6564bbe3b) (which you can do now before voting opens).

When voting begins you login and can add 10 votes for Sean and Jenna, #TeamSweetandSpicey.

Only votes cast during the live show so consider setting a calendar reminder for 8pm ET. For those in Mountain or Pacific time zones you can still vote but it has to happen while the show is live on the east coast (6-8pm CT and 5-7pm PT).

All the information on making an ABC account and other FAQs are [here](https://seanspicer.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d009170fb4604b5974b5521bd&id=10a6bea82a&e=f6564bbe3b).
---
|

Thanks,

_Sean_

name: =?utf-8?Q?Janet=20Barresi?=
subject: Video: Honoring our veterans
body:
On this Veterans Day, I remember my father. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/134b20dd62b7/calling-all-patriots-12040025?e=795c6d9796)
---
| | [ ](https://janet2020.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a78cce64304e05c22b2864ace&id=a31d0e81a7&e=795c6d9796)
---
| | [ ](https://janet2020.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a78cce64304e05c22b2864ace&id=cdf03e40d3&e=795c6d9796)
---
| Peter,

As I run for office, our armed forces and the defense of our great nation are front and center in my mind. Military service has never been a theoretical part of my family. It's always been an integral part of it.

On this Veterans Day, in my heart is the memory of my father, Jim Costello who served in the Navy in the Pacific theater during WWII. My brother John Costello is a veteran of Vietnam while serving in the army. And my cousin Ken Vogelsang sadly lost his life in Vietnam.

I am proud to know so many other individuals that have served in WWII, Korea and Vietnam. I know that they are not veterans but I also am grateful for the service of our extended family and close friends who are currently serving.

I can’t express enough my gratitude, and the gratitude of the whole country for every American veteran’s service, dedication, courage, and grit.

They willingly serve to protect all of us so we can enjoy the freedoms this country provides.
---
| [ ](https://janet2020.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a78cce64304e05c22b2864ace&id=bdff56697b&e=795c6d9796)
---
Listen to Janet Barresi's message to our veterans.
| We have to make sure that our federal government treats the men and women who have sacrificed for our nation with the respect they deserve. If elected to Congress, I plan to do just that.

I hope you take the time this Veterans Day to thank a veteran in your life or in your community for their service to this great nation. They protected the freedoms we cherish, and we should all be grateful.

Best,

\--Janet Barresi

P.S. [**If you believe that conservatives are best positioned to help our Veterans have the tools they need to succeed after they serve our country, please consider donating to our campaign to bring Oklahoma values to Washington.**](https://janet2020.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a78cce64304e05c22b2864ace&id=fa76c3d9ec&e=795c6d9796)
